page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 360.0 GB of dedicated memory, resulting in a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall in the bottom 25%. Conversely, multi-core capabilities are strong, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth (both read-only and write large block) ranking in the top 25%. Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong performance, reaching 62.03 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the n1-standard-96 is its reliance on older Intel Xeon architecture, which limits single-threaded execution speed and results in poor text generation speeds for large language models. However, the high density of 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM offers a cost-efficient profile for highly parallelized workloads that do not require the latest instruction sets or local NVMe storage.

This server configuration is suitable for multi-threaded applications, memory-intensive database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It is also applicable for prompt processing in small to large language models, though it is not suited for real-time text generation or GPU-accelerated machine learning tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs and 360.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a distinct contrast: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression, rank in the bottom 25%, while multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and memory bandwidth, rank in the top 25%. Redis SET operations are also strong, achieving 62.03 million operations per second. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized workloads due to its high resource density, despite the single-core performance limitations of its older CPU architecture. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 360 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, but weak single-core execution.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 360 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, multi-core x86_64 server offering strong parallel processing and memory bandwidth for distributed workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Weak single-core performance and slow text generation for language models
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
